metrics.title: Skater WAR forecast
metrics.headline: 14% sharper than “last year repeats”
metrics.detail: Next-season WAR MAE 0.460 vs 0.532 naive, out of sample over 7,884 player-seasons.

metrics.title: Team WAR → standings
metrics.headline: r ≈ 0.85
metrics.detail: Summed player WAR explains (same-season) a team's points; validated across the shift-data era (2010-present). Walk-forward (prior-season team WAR → next-season goal diff) r ≈ 0.50 vs naive last-season-goal-diff r ≈ 0.54 over 459 team-season pairs — no team-level predictive edge over naive; the system's value is player-level attribution. Published because the negative result is the point.

metrics.title: Trade grades
metrics.headline: Zero-sum by construction
metrics.detail: Across 3,000 graded sides the value credited and debited sums to -0.0 WAR (residual is a known handful of 3-team deals).

metrics.title: Draft board (rounds 2-7)
metrics.headline: +1.12 WAR7 over slot
metrics.detail: Top-decile age-adjusted producers taken in rounds 2-7 hit WAR7≥2 at 44% vs a 7% slot expectation (2011-2019 backtest).

metrics.title: What we DON'T claim
metrics.headline: Junior shot-quality: no edge
metrics.detail: Tested CHL play-by-play shot quality against points-per-game for predicting NHL outcomes — no incremental signal, so we didn't ship it.

market.headline: Within 0.004 of the Vegas closing line
market.summary: We scored our in-season game model head-to-head against the real Vegas closing moneyline on every game of the 2025-26 season -- the sharpest public number there is. Over 1,083 games we land within four thousandths of a nat of the market on log-loss. Almost no public model reaches the closing line; we sit a half-step behind it (a naive Elo baseline runs ≈.693 log-loss on the same games; we land at .6859 vs the close's .6822).
market.model: Validated winner: war_diff + xg_form + b2b_diff + elo_diff, logistic, fit leave-one-season-out so every prediction is out of sample.
market.market_source: The Odds API historical snapshots -- consensus de-vigged h2h across US books at the latest pre-game snapshot (no in-progress look-ahead).
market.note: The market's small, consistent edge is the real-time information we don't yet ingest -- confirmed starting goalie, late scratches, sharp money. Closing that gap (a game-time starter + injury feed) is the next frontier, not a modeling flaw.
market.verdict_sub: market-grade band — no public-side edge claimed
market.clv_sub: of games on the closing-line side (±3.0% at n=1,083 — within noise; shown for transparency)
